steps.title: Rewrite the half-power as a square root
steps.content: A rational exponent of $\tfrac{1}{2}$ is the principal square root: for any $a\ge 0$, $a^{1/2}=\sqrt{a}$. Applying this with $a=39y$:$$(39y)^{1/2}=\sqrt{39y}.$$

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A: $39\sqrt{y}$ would equal $\sqrt{39^2\cdot y}=\sqrt{1521y}$, not $\sqrt{39y}$.
  • B: $\sqrt{39}\cdot y$ equals $\sqrt{39\cdot y^2}=\sqrt{39y^2}$, not $\sqrt{39y}$.
  • C: $\sqrt{39y}$ — identical to the expression we derived.
  • D: $\sqrt{(39y)^2}=|39y|=39y$ (since $y\gt 1$), which is the original expression squared, not its square root.
That matches Choice C — $\sqrt{39y}$.
